bullets.text: Finance ministers and top bankers have expressed serious concerns about the Mythos AI model, citing its unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.
More than £1 billion has been pledged for Sudan at a conference in Berlin, exceeding the funding target amidst a deepening humanitarian crisis.
The Swiss National Bank has cut interest rates by half a point to 0.5%, a move that saw the dollar jump 0.5% against the franc.
Parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after a collision with a police car, shared emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing began.
Companies and directors were fined $7 million for operating a payday lending scheme that breached credit rules and generated over $91 million in fees and charges.
Australian soldiers have reportedly told prosecutors that former SAS corporal Ben Roberts-Smith ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, according to court documents.
President Trump stated that the war in Iran "should be ending pretty soon" as a ceasefire in Lebanon began, suggesting potential US-Iran talks this weekend.
French President Macron and UK Labour leader Starmer are expected to hold a summit to discuss a plan for securing the Strait of Hormuz, with a briefing to Donald Trump planned afterward.
World leaders are reportedly turning to China's Xi Jinping as the Iran war continues, following stalled peace talks between the US and Iran.
